description: HTTP status-checking library with integration gate. Exercises two-phase build: mocked TDD for core logic, then gated integration validation against real endpoints.

environment.notes: Integration stories require STATUS_CHECKER_API_URL and STATUS_CHECKER_DB_URL env vars pointing to real endpoints.

userStories.title: Check a single endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I can check the health of a single HTTP endpoint so that I know whether it is up or down.
acceptanceCriteria: checkEndpoint returns { name, url, status, healthy, latencyMs, checkedAt }
healthy is true when response status matches expectedStatus (default 200)
healthy is false when response status does not match
latencyMs is a positive number representing milliseconds
userStories.notes: Use dependency injection — accept a fetcher function so tests can pass a mock.

userStories.title: Batch check multiple endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I can check multiple endpoints concurrently so that I get all results quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: checkAll accepts an array of endpoints and returns an array of results
All checks run concurrently (Promise.all or equivalent)
A failing endpoint does not prevent other checks from completing
Results array has one entry per input endpoint

userStories.title: Summarise check results
userStories.description: As a user, I can get a summary of all check results so that I can see overall health at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: summarise returns { total, healthy, unhealthy, endpoints }
total equals the number of results
healthy counts results where healthy is true
Each endpoint entry includes a human-readable status line

userStories.title: Threshold alerting
userStories.description: As a user, I can define alerting rules so that I get warnings when latency is too high or required endpoints are unhealthy.
acceptanceCriteria: getAlerts returns alert strings for endpoints exceeding maxLatencyMs
getAlerts returns alert strings when requiredHealthy endpoints are unhealthy
Returns empty array when all rules pass
Each alert string identifies the endpoint and violation

userStories.title: Verify real endpoint connectivity
userStories.description: Integration validation: verify that the status checker can reach real HTTP endpoints over the network and return accurate results.
acceptanceCriteria: checkEndpoint with real fetch against STATUS_CHECKER_API_URL returns a valid result
checkEndpoint with real fetch against STATUS_CHECKER_DB_URL returns a valid result
Reported status codes match actual HTTP responses
userStories.notes: Integration story — uses real HTTP, no mocks.
userStories.gate: Set STATUS_CHECKER_API_URL and STATUS_CHECKER_DB_URL environment variables pointing to reachable HTTP endpoints before continuing. Any URL that returns an HTTP response will work (e.g. https://httpbin.org/status/200).

userStories.title: End-to-end smoke test
userStories.description: Integration validation: run a full batch check and summary against real endpoints to verify the entire pipeline works end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: checkAll with real fetch and both configured endpoints returns results for both
summarise produces correct counts matching real endpoint states
getAlerts with a 5000ms latency threshold returns no alerts for reachable endpoints
userStories.notes: Integration story — full pipeline smoke test.
